1.1.title: Course Policies
1.1.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 1.1 — Course Policies

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course clearly states policies that support student success (academic honesty, withdrawal, work submission, communication, participation) and makes them accessible to students.

WHERE TO LOOK: Syllabus, Orientation/Getting Started module, and any dedicated policy pages.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Policies addressing academic honesty/integrity
- Withdrawal or drop policy
- Work submission policy (deadlines, late work, grace periods)
- Communication policy (contact methods, response times)
- Participation policy (engagement expectations)
- Policies clearly labeled and easy to locate
- Policies written in student-friendly language
- Policies appear in the syllabus, orientation module, or dedicated policy pages

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Important policies entirely missing (e.g., no mention of academic honesty at all)
- Policies so difficult to locate that students would struggle to find them
- Expectations fundamentally unclear or contradictory

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No course policies found, or most required policy areas (academic honesty, withdrawal, submission, communication, participation) are absent.
- approaching: Fewer than three of the five policy areas are addressed, OR policies are present but fundamentally unclear or contradictory.
- aligned: Course policies supporting student success (academic honesty, withdrawal, work submission, communication, participation) are clearly stated and easy to find. Policies may appear in the syllabus, an orientation module, dedicated pages, or a combination — presence in at least one well-organized, accessible location is sufficient. Placeholder text for campus-specific details (e.g., phone numbers, office names) does not disqualify alignment if the policy intent and expectations are clear.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND relevant policies are also included within units or activities where they are most useful, helping students apply expectations as they move through the course.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "1.1", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

1.2.title: Equitable Use of Technology
1.2.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 1.2 — Equitable Use of Technology

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the instructor explains how required technology supports learning, provides guidance for accessing tools, and offers information about obtaining tech support.

WHERE TO LOOK: Syllabus, Orientation/Getting Started module, and assignment instructions where technology is used.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- A list or explanation of required technology tools
- Description of how tools support learning activities
- Instructions for accessing the technology (links, downloads, login info)
- Information about where to obtain technical support
- Browser requirements or device recommendations
- Mention of mobile app availability or alternative access methods

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Required technology never mentioned or explained
- Students given no guidance on how to access tools
- Technical support information completely absent

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No explanation of required technology found in the course.
- approaching: Technology is mentioned but students are given no guidance on how to access it AND no tech support information is provided.
- aligned: The instructor explains how the required technology supports learning and provides guidance for accessing the tools and tech support. Note: Using institutionally provided and approved tools (such as Canvas, Zoom, or other LMS-integrated tools) implicitly meets accessibility requirements — an explicit accessibility statement is NOT required for alignment. Providing browser/device requirements, download links, or mobile app information counts as access guidance.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND offers flexibility or alternative options to support equitable access; students are invited to provide anonymous feedback about their technology experience.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "1.2", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

1.3.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
1.3.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 1.3 — Artificial Intelligence (AI)

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course includes a student-centered, course-specific AI policy that clearly states expectations for how AI may or may not be used, and whether the instructor explains their own AI use in the course.

WHERE TO LOOK: Syllabus, course policies page, Orientation/Start Here module, and assignment instructions where AI expectations may differ.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- A course-specific AI policy (not just a generic institutional academic honesty statement)
- Clear explanation of when AI tools may or may not be used
- Guidance for both student and instructor use of AI
- Connection between AI use expectations and academic integrity
- Policy placed in locations easy for students to find

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- No AI policy provided
- Policy is only the institution's general academic honesty statement, not course-specific
- Expectations for AI use unclear or ambiguous
- Students not told whether AI use is allowed, restricted, or conditional
- Policy difficult to locate

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No AI policy found in the course.
- approaching: Some mention of AI but the policy is generic, unclear, or missing key components (student use guidance, instructor use disclosure, or connection to integrity).
- aligned: A student-centered, course-specific AI policy is clearly stated, easy to find, and includes clear expectations for how AI may or may not be used. The instructor explains if/how they will be using AI within the course.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND AI guidance is integrated into specific course activities and assignments, with any instructor AI use clearly indicated at point of use.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "1.3", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

1.4.title: Data Privacy and Security
1.4.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 1.4 — Data Privacy and Security

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course provides students with guidance on safe technology use and references institutional privacy/security policies or practices.

WHERE TO LOOK: Syllabus, Orientation/Getting Started module, tech support pages, and instructions for activities that use external tools.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Link or reference to institution's privacy and/or security policies
- Guidance for safe technology use (e.g., protect credentials, use secure connections, log out on shared computers, set videos to private/unlisted)
- Information about privacy considerations when external tools require account creation
- References to institutional data handling (e.g., FERPA compliance, LMS security)
- Placement in a location students can find

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- No privacy or security information of any kind in the course
- External tools required with no mention of privacy whatsoever
- Students given no guidance about safe practices online

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No privacy/security information or guidance found in the course.
- approaching: Only a passing mention of privacy exists with no actionable student guidance.
- aligned: The course provides students with guidance on safe technology use and protecting their data when using course-related tools or platforms, and references or links to institutional privacy and security policies are present (or the course uses only institutionally managed tools where privacy is handled at the platform level). Guidance such as setting YouTube videos to private/unlisted, using secure connections, protecting login credentials, or following institutional security practices all count as valid privacy guidance.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND provides opportunities/resources for students to understand digital privacy best practices and learn about their rights; clearly communicates how student data is collected, used, and protected.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "1.4", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

1.5.title: Student Resources and Support
1.5.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 1.5 — Student Resources and Support

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course provides clearly labeled, easy-to-find links to institutional support services including disability services, counseling, tutoring, library, basic needs, and technology help.

WHERE TO LOOK: Syllabus, Orientation/Getting Started module, or a dedicated Student Resources page.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Links to disability/accessibility services
- Links to tutoring services
- Links to counseling or student support services
- Links to library resources
- Links to technology help or help desk
- Links to basic needs support (food, housing, emergency assistance)
- Clearly labeled resource links that are easy to find
- A dedicated page organizing student support resources

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Links to institutional support services missing
- Services mentioned but no links provided
- Resources difficult to locate
- Only a limited number of services included; key resources missing

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No links to institutional support services found.
- approaching: Some support links present but missing key services (disability, counseling, tutoring, library, basic needs, or tech help) or links are hard to locate.
- aligned: Links to institutional support services (disability services, counseling, tutoring, library, basic needs, and technology help) are clearly labeled and easy for students to find.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND institutional support resources are purposefully integrated into relevant parts of the course; the course highlights how services support student success and proactively reminds students to use them throughout the term.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "1.5", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

1.6.title: Learning Support
1.6.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 1.6 — Learning Support

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course includes support resources within modules and activities to help students successfully complete assignments (tips, reminders, tutorials, helpful links).

WHERE TO LOOK: Module pages, assignment instructions, discussions, and announcements tied to course activities.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Tips or reminders placed within assignments or modules
- Links to tutorials or guides that clarify challenging concepts
- Optional enrichment resources supporting deeper learning
- Learning support materials integrated throughout the course (not only in the syllabus)

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- No learning support resources included in the course
- Resources appear only in the syllabus and not within course activities
- Support materials rare or isolated rather than integrated throughout
- Optional resources unclear or appear to be required

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No learning support resources (tips, reminders, helpful links) found within course activities.
- approaching: Some support resources present but isolated or only in the syllabus; not integrated into modules/activities where students need them.
- aligned: The course includes support resources to assist students in successfully completing assignments and activities, such as tips, reminders, or helpful resource links.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND provides additional resources allowing students to explore content more deeply, placed within units or activities where they best support extended learning.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "1.6", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

2.1.title: Structure and Navigation
2.1.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 2.1 — Structure and Navigation

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether Canvas tools and modules are used consistently to organize content, provide a clear structure that is easy to navigate, and employ accessible navigation and formatting practices.

WHERE TO LOOK: Home page, Modules area, course navigation menu, and Start Here/Orientation module.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- A clearly labeled starting point (Start Here or Orientation module)
- Consistent module organization and naming conventions
- Streamlined course navigation (unnecessary links removed)
- Instructions or guidance explaining how to navigate the course
- Consistent format across modules (overview, materials, activities, assessments)

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- No clear starting point for students
- Modules inconsistently organized or labeled
- Too many navigation links visible, creating confusion
- Instructions for navigating the course unclear or absent

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No consistent structure or navigation guidance found; course lacks a clear starting point.
- approaching: Some structure present but inconsistent module organization, unclear naming, or missing navigation guidance.
- aligned: Canvas tools and modules are used in a consistent way to organize content and help students move through the course. The course has a clear structure that is easy to navigate and uses accessible navigation and formatting practices.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND the course is designed to be inclusive and easy to navigate across multiple devices; helpful technology recommendations are provided throughout.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "2.1", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

2.2.title: Module Objectives
2.2.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 2.2 — Module Objectives

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether module learning objectives are consistently placed and describe specific, measurable outcomes that align to course-level objectives.

WHERE TO LOOK: Beginning of each module, module overview pages, or module introductions.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Objectives present in each module
- Objectives written using measurable action verbs (Bloom's taxonomy)
- Objectives aligned with course-level learning outcomes
- Consistent placement of objectives across modules
- Objectives written from the student perspective (what students will be able to do)

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Module objectives missing from some modules
- Objectives vague or not measurable (e.g., 'understand' or 'learn about' without specificity)
- Objectives do not clearly connect to course-level objectives
- Objectives difficult for students to locate

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: Module objectives not found or present in very few modules.
- approaching: Some objectives present but inconsistently placed, vague, not measurable, or not clearly aligned to course-level outcomes.
- aligned: Module learning objectives are consistently placed and describe specific, measurable outcomes that align to the course-level objectives.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND objectives are written in student-centered language that explains what students will be able to do, why it matters, and how it connects to future learning or real-life situations.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "2.2", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

2.3.title: Module Alignment
2.3.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 2.3 — Module Alignment

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether module content and assessments align with the module objectives and are adequate to support achievement of those objectives.

WHERE TO LOOK: Module pages, assignments, discussions, and assessments within each module; compare against stated module objectives.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Learning materials that support the knowledge/skills described in objectives
- Activities that allow students to practice those skills
- Assessments that measure the learning outcomes described in objectives
- Clear connection between what students are asked to do and what objectives state

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Module activities not clearly connected to module objectives
- Assessments measure skills different from those in objectives
- Modules include content not supporting stated objectives
- Module does not provide sufficient activities to support achievement of objectives

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No discernible connection between module content/assessments and stated objectives, or objectives are absent making alignment impossible to assess.
- approaching: Some alignment exists but notable gaps where assessments don't match objectives or content doesn't adequately support stated outcomes.
- aligned: Module content and assessments align with the module objectives and are adequate to support achievement.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND module content and assessments are intentionally mapped to each module objective with connections clearly explained to students.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "2.3", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

2.4.title: Canvas Tools and Apps
2.4.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 2.4 — Canvas Tools and Apps

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether Canvas tools and institutionally approved apps are used intentionally to ensure accessibility, reduce learning labor, and streamline student access to materials and activities.

WHERE TO LOOK: Modules area, course navigation menu, assignment settings, discussions, quizzes, and Canvas pages.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Modules used to organize course content and activities in sequence
- Resources embedded within Canvas pages (not requiring extra clicks/downloads without context)
- Assignments and discussions with clear instructions and due dates
- Correct Canvas tool used for each purpose (Assignments for graded work, Discussions for interaction, Quizzes for assessments)
- Institutionally approved apps used to support activities

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Course content includes many unnecessary links or scattered files
- Materials scattered across Files, Pages, and external links without structure
- Assignments or activities lack clear instructions or due dates
- External tools used without institutional approval or accessibility consideration
- Students asked to submit work through Inbox or email instead of Assignments

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: Canvas tools not used effectively; content disorganized with no clear module structure or appropriate tool usage.
- approaching: Some effective use of Canvas tools but inconsistencies (e.g., some content outside modules, tools used for wrong purpose, missing instructions).
- aligned: Canvas tools and institutionally approved apps are used to ensure accessibility, reduce the labor intensity of learning, and streamline access to materials and activities for students.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND Canvas tools/apps support student voices and encourage connections with classmates, the college, the community, or professionals in the field.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "2.4", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

2.5.title: Use of Multimedia
2.5.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 2.5 — Use of Multimedia

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course consistently uses multiple types of accessible media (text, audio, video, images) to support learning.

WHERE TO LOOK: Module pages, readings, assignments, discussions, and instructional materials throughout the course.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Use of text, images, audio, or video to present course concepts
- Multimedia materials that support understanding of content (not just decoration)
- Accessible media (captioned videos, images with alt text)
- Content from diverse authors, speakers, or cultural contexts
- Media directly connected to learning objectives

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Content presented almost entirely through text with no multimedia
- Multimedia used in only one or two modules
- Media is entirely inaccessible (no captions, no alt text anywhere)

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: Course content relies almost exclusively on one format (typically text) with no meaningful use of multimedia.
- approaching: Some multimedia present but used in fewer than half the modules, OR media is largely inaccessible.
- aligned: The course consistently uses different types of media that meet accessibility standards — such as text, audio, video, and images — to support student learning. Diversity in content is demonstrated through inclusion of varied perspectives, authors, examples, or cultural contexts within the subject matter. An explicit diversity statement is NOT required if the course materials themselves reflect diverse voices and perspectives. Content from diverse authors (different genders, backgrounds, cultures) or examples drawn from varied cultural contexts satisfies the diversity component.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND facilitates student access to a wide range of low-burden media used creatively and intentionally to support learning and deepen understanding.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "2.5", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

2.6.title: Guidance for Working with Content
2.6.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 2.6 — Guidance for Working with Content

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course provides instructions guiding students in how to engage with content, giving them direction beyond simply listing resources.

WHERE TO LOOK: Module overview pages, Canvas pages containing readings or videos, assignment instructions, discussion prompts.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Instructions placed near readings, videos, or external resources
- Suggestions to take notes, reflect, or focus on specific concepts
- Explanations of how content connects to assignments or discussions
- Directions guiding students on what ideas/concepts to focus on
- Context for why content is included and how it will be used
- Introductory text before readings that frames what students will learn
- Weekly overviews that contextualize the module content

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Resources provided with zero explanation or framing
- Content is only a list of links with no surrounding text
- Students never told how any content connects to any activity

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No guidance provided for engaging with course content; resources listed without any context or instructions.
- approaching: Content pages exist but the vast majority provide no framing, context, or direction for students.
- aligned: The course provides instructions that guide students in engaging with content. This can include suggestions to consider taking notes, guidance on what to focus on, framing text that introduces readings or videos with context, explanations of how content connects to upcoming assignments, or weekly overviews that set expectations. Phrases like 'Read through the following and consider taking notes' or 'Give particular attention to...' or 'This section covers X which connects to this week's assignment' all qualify as valid content engagement guidance.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND instructions explicitly explain how content supports learning; instructions anticipate common challenges and guide students through them.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "2.6", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

3.1.title: Initial Course Contact
3.1.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 3.1 — Initial Course Contact

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the instructor provides a welcome message that gives students key information needed to start the course, including first-week requirements, technology overview, alternative access options, and links to Canvas support.

WHERE TO LOOK: Start Here or Orientation module, Welcome or Getting Started page, syllabus, and announcements.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- A welcome message introducing the course
- Clear instructions for the first steps students should take
- References to first-week activities or expectations
- Basic information about tools or support resources
- Warm, supportive tone that helps students feel comfortable starting

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- No welcome message included
- Message does not explain how to begin the course
- First-week tasks or expectations missing
- Support or access information not provided

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No welcome message or initial course contact found.
- approaching: A welcome message exists but is missing key elements (first steps, first-week expectations, or support/access information).
- aligned: The instructor provides a welcome message that gives students the key information they need to start the course, including first-week requirements, a brief overview of technology used, alternative access options (if needed), and links to Canvas support.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND the instructor sends more than one welcome message (e.g., a pre-course introduction and a first-day reminder); messages are student-centered and foster a sense of belonging.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "3.1", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

3.2.title: Instructor-Initiated Interaction
3.2.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 3.2 — Instructor-Initiated Interaction

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course includes a clear explanation of when and how the instructor will initiate regular interaction with students.

WHERE TO LOOK: Syllabus, Start Here/Orientation module, a page labeled Instructor Interaction or Communication, and course design elements (weekly overview pages, discussion facilitation notes, feedback commitments).

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- A clear explanation of instructor communication methods
- Defined frequency of interaction (e.g., weekly announcements, weekly videos)
- Stated response times for messages and feedback
- Description of instructor presence in discussions
- Weekly overview pages that demonstrate planned instructor engagement
- Feedback timelines stated in a communication plan or grading page

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- No explanation anywhere of how the instructor will interact with students
- Course has no communication plan, no stated response times, and no mention of instructor-initiated contact

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No explanation of how or when the instructor will initiate interaction with students.
- approaching: Only vague or passing mention of instructor communication with no specifics about frequency, method, or timing.
- aligned: The course includes a clear explanation of when and how the instructor will initiate regular interaction with students. This explanation may appear in a communication plan page, syllabus, orientation module, or dedicated page. Evidence 'throughout the course design' means the course structure supports the stated plan (e.g., weekly overview pages exist if weekly contact is promised, discussion prompts exist if discussion participation is promised). The plan does NOT need to be restated in every module — a single, clear, comprehensive communication plan that describes multiple interaction types (announcements, videos, discussion presence, feedback) is sufficient for alignment.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND the explanation facilitates student understanding of why RSI matters; provides deeper interaction throughout the course.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "3.2", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

3.3.title: Student-Initiated Interaction
3.3.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 3.3 — Student-Initiated Interaction

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether students can easily locate instructor contact information and details about regular opportunities for interaction during the course, including expected response times.

WHERE TO LOOK: Syllabus, Orientation/Getting Started module, homepage, or a communication/contact page.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Instructor name and preferred contact method
- Clear directions for how to contact the instructor
- Expected response times for messages
- Office hours or other interaction opportunities
- A Course Questions discussion forum or similar structured way for students to ask questions

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Contact information missing or difficult to locate
- Response times not clearly communicated
- Contact information appears in only one location
- Students not encouraged to contact the instructor

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: Instructor contact information and response time expectations not found.
- approaching: Some contact information present but missing response times, difficult to locate, or lacks clear direction on how to reach the instructor.
- aligned: Students can easily locate instructor contact information and details about regular opportunities for interaction during the course and expected response times.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND multiple ways to contact the instructor are provided; students are actively encouraged to reach out at different points throughout the course or within relevant assignments.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "3.3", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

3.4.title: Facilitation of Student Interaction
3.4.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 3.4 — Facilitation of Student Interaction

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course includes opportunities for student-to-student interaction focused on course content, and whether the instructor's role in facilitating these interactions is explained.

WHERE TO LOOK: Discussions, peer review assignments, group work, collaborative activities within modules, and any page explaining the instructor's facilitation approach.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Discussion activities requiring engagement with peers (not just posting)
- Peer review or collaborative assignments
- Clear instructions guiding how students should interact with each other
- An explanation of how the instructor will guide or support student interaction
- Prompts that require students to build on, question, or respond to classmates' ideas

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Students complete activities independently without interacting with classmates
- Discussion activities require posts but do not encourage meaningful responses
- Instructor's role in facilitating interaction not explained
- No structured opportunities for student-to-student engagement about content

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No opportunities for student-to-student interaction found, or instructor facilitation role not addressed.
- approaching: Some interaction opportunities exist (e.g., basic discussions) but lacking meaningful peer engagement requirements or no explanation of instructor's facilitation role.
- aligned: The course includes opportunities for student-to-student interaction focused on course content; the instructor's role in facilitating these interactions is explained.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND the instructor demonstrates active facilitation of regular student-to-student interaction through structured activities and thoughtful prompts with clear guidelines that foster engagement and inclusivity.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "3.4", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

4.1.title: Variety & Frequency
4.1.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 4.1 — Variety & Frequency

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course provides relevant formative and summative assessments throughout the term, offering different ways for students to demonstrate learning and stay engaged.

WHERE TO LOOK: Discussions, assignments, quizzes, projects, and other graded activities within course modules.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- A combination of formative assessments (practice quizzes, reflections, drafts, low-stakes discussions) and summative assessments (essays, projects, exams, portfolios)
- Multiple ways for students to demonstrate learning (discussions, quizzes, written work, projects, presentations)
- Assessment opportunities distributed across modules/units (not concentrated at beginning or end)
- Activities helping students practice before major assignments (scaffolding)
- Mix of lower-order (recall, identify) and higher-order (analyze, evaluate, create) thinking

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Course relies primarily on one type of assessment (only quizzes, only discussions)
- Assessments appear only at the end of the course
- Students have few opportunities to practice before major assignments
- No formative assessment opportunities

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: Very limited assessment types or assessments not distributed throughout the term.
- approaching: Some variety exists but either limited in type (e.g., mostly one format) or not distributed regularly throughout the course.
- aligned: The course provides relevant formative and summative assessments throughout the term, offering different ways for students to show what they know and stay engaged.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND most assessments support higher-order thinking, connect to real-world situations, and promote inclusive, engaging learning experiences.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "4.1", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

4.2.title: Scoring Criteria
4.2.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 4.2 — Scoring Criteria

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether assessments include a clear explanation of how student work will be evaluated, such as rubrics or defined expectations.

WHERE TO LOOK: Assignment instructions, discussion prompts, quizzes, and any attached rubrics.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Rubrics attached to assignments or discussions (with criteria, performance levels, points)
- Clearly described grading criteria within assignment instructions
- Expectations explaining how points or performance levels are determined
- Guidance helping students understand how to use rubrics to prepare work

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Assessments do not include clear grading criteria
- Expectations unclear or difficult to locate
- Students complete assignments without guidance on evaluation
- Rubrics or criteria missing from major assignments

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No scoring criteria, rubrics, or defined expectations found for assessments.
- approaching: Some scoring criteria present but inconsistent across assessments, or criteria are vague/unclear.
- aligned: Assessments include a clear explanation of how student work will be evaluated, such as rubrics or defined expectations.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND assessments consistently include detailed rubrics or criteria plus examples or models of student work to clarify expectations and support success.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

4.3.title: Assessment Instructions
4.3.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 4.3 — Assessment Instructions

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether accessible instructions clearly explain what students need to do to complete assignments successfully and provide relevant resources.

WHERE TO LOOK: Assignments, discussions, quizzes, and other graded activities in course modules.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Instructions describing the purpose of the assignment
- Step-by-step directions for completing the task
- Submission requirements and due dates clearly stated
- Links to grading criteria or rubrics
- Relevant resources supporting task completion (readings, tutorials, writing center, examples)

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Instructions unclear or incomplete
- Students not told how to submit their work
- Important details (due dates, format, grading criteria) missing
- Instructions difficult to follow or poorly organized

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: Assessment instructions missing or so incomplete that students cannot determine what to do.
- approaching: Instructions present but missing key elements (purpose, submission details, due dates, or relevant resources).
- aligned: Accessible instructions clearly explain what students need to do to complete the assignment successfully and provide relevant resources.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND instructions are offered in multiple formats (written, visual, or audio) to support different learning needs; instructions offer alternative options to reduce barriers.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

4.4.title: Assessment Feedback
4.4.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 4.4 — Assessment Feedback

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course clearly explains how and when students will receive feedback on their work.

WHERE TO LOOK: Syllabus, Start Here/Orientation module, a dedicated feedback/grading page, and within assessment instructions.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- A statement describing expected feedback timelines (e.g., 'within 3-5 days after due date')
- Information about how feedback will be provided (comments, rubrics, annotations, audio/video)
- Students told where to find feedback in Canvas
- A dedicated page explaining the feedback process
- Feedback timelines mentioned in a communication plan or grading page

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- No explanation anywhere of when or how feedback will be provided
- Students have no way to know when to expect feedback
- No information about feedback format or location anywhere in the course

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No explanation of how or when students will receive feedback found anywhere in the course.
- approaching: Feedback is vaguely mentioned but no timeline, format, or location details are provided.
- aligned: The course clearly explains how and when students will receive feedback on both formative and summative assessments. This information may appear in a centralized location such as a dedicated 'Finding Grades and Feedback' page, communication plan, syllabus, or orientation module. It does NOT need to be repeated within every individual assessment's instructions — a clear, accessible, centralized explanation that covers feedback timelines, methods, and location is sufficient for alignment.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND provides clear instructions for how students can access feedback in Canvas; may include opportunities to use feedback to improve future work.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "4.4", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

4.5.title: Learner Self-Reflection
4.5.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 4.5 — Learner Self-Reflection

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course provides multiple opportunities for students to reflect on their learning and receive formative feedback from the instructor as appropriate.

WHERE TO LOOK: Assignments, discussions, journals, quizzes, or brief prompts within modules.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- Reflection prompts following assignments or modules
- Learning journals or progress reflections
- Self-assessment questions embedded within activities
- 'Muddiest point' activities or exit tickets
- Mid-course or mid-semester reflections
- Multiple reflection opportunities distributed throughout the course (not just one)

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- Reflection opportunities missing entirely
- Reflection included only once rather than throughout the course
- Reflection prompts unclear or not connected to learning
- No instructor feedback on reflection activities

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No self-reflection opportunities found in the course.
- approaching: One or very few reflection activities present, or reflection prompts are unclear/disconnected from learning goals.
- aligned: The course provides multiple opportunities for students to reflect on their learning and receive formative feedback from the instructor as appropriate.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND structured reflection opportunities help students connect their effort to results and plan for improvement.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "4.5", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.

4.6.title: Learner Feedback Survey
4.6.evaluation_prompt:
ELEMENT 4.6 — Learner Feedback Survey

WHAT THIS EVALUATES: Whether the course provides an anonymous opportunity near the end for learners to give feedback to the instructor regarding course design and/or content.

WHERE TO LOOK: Final module or closing activity near the end of the course; may also be referenced in the syllabus.

EVIDENCE SIGNALS (indicative, not exhaustive):
- A survey placed near the end of the course
- Anonymous feedback questions about course design or learning activities
- Instructions inviting students to share their feedback
- Survey covering course organization, activity effectiveness, workload/pacing, or overall experience

FAILURE SIGNALS:
- No opportunity for students to provide course feedback
- Survey is not anonymous
- Feedback opportunity occurs too early in the course (not near end)
- Students not clearly invited to participate

RATING SCALE:
- incomplete: No anonymous feedback survey or opportunity for course feedback found.
- approaching: A feedback mechanism exists but is not anonymous, not placed near end of course, or does not clearly invite student participation.
- aligned: At the end of the course, learners have the opportunity to give anonymous feedback to the instructor regarding course design and/or course content.
- exceptional: Meets ALL aligned criteria AND provides opportunities throughout the course (not just at the end) to give anonymous feedback regarding course design and/or content.
- not_evaluable: Use ONLY when the supplied Course Content is empty, contains the literal no-content marker, or is wholly unrelated/unusable so that this element cannot be judged at all. Do NOT use not_evaluable merely because the criterion or positive evidence is absent.

DECISION RULES (apply before choosing a rating):
- Treat any substantive Course Content supplied for this scope as sufficient to evaluate. If the expected feature is absent from substantive content, choose incomplete, not not_evaluable.
- Choose aligned when one or more credible evidence signals demonstrate the core intent of the element. Evidence signals are indicative examples, not a checklist; do not require every signal.
- Minor omissions, wording differences, placement differences, or optional enhancements may be listed as missing_items or suggestions but do not prevent aligned when the core intent is met.
- Choose approaching only when a material gap weakens the core intent; choose incomplete when the core feature is absent.
- If a truncation note is present, judge the available evidence normally. Truncation alone never justifies not_evaluable.

RESPONSE DISCIPLINE:
- Use no more than 2 short, decisive evidence quotes. Incomplete and not_evaluable may use zero quotes.
- Return no more than 3 brief missing_items. Keep suggested_fix and reasoning to no more than 2 short sentences each.

OUTPUT: Return JSON: {"element_id": "4.6", "rating": "<rating>", "confidence": <0.0-1.0>, "evidence_quotes": [{"quote": "<verbatim>", "page_id": "<id>", "page_title": "<title>"}], "missing_items": ["<what is absent>"], "suggested_fix": "<concrete action>", "reasoning": "<brief explanation>"}. Every non-incomplete rating requires at least one verbatim evidence_quote from the course content.
